frameworks.name: fMRI brain dynamics / co-activation patterns (CAPs)
frameworks.one_line: Resting-state dynamics characterized via reoccurring co-activation patterns and their temporal properties.

frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: Anodal tDCS as causal perturbation tool to modulate cortical excitability and brain-state dynamics.

core_question: Can repeated tDCS to rDLPFC and lOFC causally modulate the temporal properties of fMRI co-activation patterns?
key_assumptions: Temporal properties of CAPs index meaningful aspects of brain-state dynamics
tDCS enhances neuronal excitability of the targeted cortical region
Sham tDCS provides a valid causal control
key_findings: Two reoccurring CAPs identified using rDLPFC and lOFC as seeds
Active tDCS significantly decreased occurrence rate of one CAP and increased that of another during the first 6 min
tDCS-associated changes persisted across subsequent testing sessions
interpretations: Enhanced excitability of rDLPFC/lOFC causally shifts the temporal occupancy of resting-state brain dynamics
tDCS is a viable tool to manipulate fMRI brain dynamics, opening a path to probe disrupted dynamics in neuropsychiatric disorders
